reasoning_model_1: The audio content strictly follows the script 'Get out of the way, don't block my path!' with perfect accuracy. The speaker adopts a clear, impatient, and stern tone, effectively capturing the 'rude' paralinguistic requirement. The emphasis on 'Get out' and the sharp delivery of 'don't block my path' (0.0s - 2.5s) demonstrate a natural and appropriate emotional expression for the command. The fluency is high, with no mechanical artifacts.
reasoning_model_2: The audio content contains a noticeable omission. The model says 'Get out the way' instead of 'Get out of the way', missing the word 'of'. This negatively impacts Content Accuracy. Regarding the paralinguistic feature, while the delivery attempts a direct tone, the grammatical error/omission makes the speech sound colloquial or rushed rather than strictly following the command's structure. The omission disrupts the natural rhythm expected from the script.
model_comparison: Model 1 is superior in all dimensions. It strictly adheres to the text script, whereas Model 2 omits the word 'of', resulting in a content error ('Get out the way'). Furthermore, Model 1 provides a more convincing and natural 'rude' tone that fits the context of the sentence. Model 2's error in content accuracy significantly lowers its score as per the evaluation criteria.
